tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: My friends are pushing to go out, but skipping another two hours of review risks my midterm score. I'll stay in and finish studying instead.
The club window closed, and I prioritized the 0.60 to 0.78 study gain instead of avoiding friction with Mina.
Getting that 0.14 gain felt like the right move, even though my energy is tanking. I'm glad I avoided the social friction to secure this progress.
The study grind is finally over; the midterm task is complete. Now I can stop pushing and just rest, even if my energy is low.
With full energy and the midterm deadline now active, I can finally focus on that 0.60 gain without worrying about sleep debt or social friction.
Messaging Mina about the club feels easy now that the midterm pressure is off, though I'm still avoiding deeper plans to keep things low-friction.
The social gain feels effortless now that the academic pressure is gone, but I'm already drifting back toward my coursework instead of deepening this connection.
The midterm task is essentially complete, which removes the primary stressor and frees up my energy for social interactions or leisure.
The 'done' message sent to Mina confirms the task is closed, but my immediate drift back to coursework shows I still prioritize academic closure over sustaining this new social ease.

desires: Secure that 'exam_prep' is permanently closed so I can stop worrying about tomorrow's performance and reclaim my mental space.
Maintain the low-friction status quo with Mina and Jae by keeping interactions light, avoiding any deep emotional conversations that might feel draining or risky right now.
Delay the laundry task indefinitely until Sunday night to preserve my current high energy for immediate leisure or another quick win, ignoring the accumulating mess.

goals.description: Physically slam the notebook shut and stand up to sever the visual tether to 'exam_prep', forcing my brain to accept the work is done so I can stop worrying about tomorrow's outcome.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means I'm already craving the dopamine hit of 'finished,' but the lingering anxiety about potential errors is draining. Closing it decisively satisfies my need for certainty without requiring me to plan ahead or analyze future consequences, which aligns with my impulsive nature.

goals.description: Send a single-word reply ('k') to Jae if they message, then immediately put the phone face down to avoid any awkward small talk that feels like an emotional drain.
goals.rationale: I value avoiding friction above all else right now. A transactional, low-effort response maintains the peace (avoidant conflict style) without demanding the energy required for deep connection or assertiveness, keeping the status quo safe.

goals.description: Consciously decide to ignore the laundry pile entirely until Sunday night, treating the mess as a temporary obstacle I can easily push aside once I've secured some leisure time.
goals.rationale: While this fits my impulsive desire to jump into a new task once stress drops, my procrastination bias and sleep discipline issues mean I'm prioritizing immediate relief over long-term order. Deferring it indefinitely preserves my current energy for a quick win or relaxation, ignoring the accumulating cost.

agent.last_reasoning: Exam prep is done (100%); pushing further risks burnout with no gain. I need to recover energy before the club outing tonight. Skipping sleep now would hurt tomorrow's performance, so I'll rest now to secure the win and avoid friction later.

goals.description: Finish the last 40% of the midterm review immediately to secure a decent grade and avoid tomorrow's anxiety.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis weights academic outcomes heavily; failing or getting a bad grade is a high-cost failure I cannot afford. My 'avoidant' nature makes me terrified of facing the professor or explaining away a low score, so I must lock this in now.

goals.description: Reply to Mina and Jae with a vague excuse about being tired/studying to ghost their invite without a detailed explanation.
goals.rationale: I value the friendship (closeness 7), but my 'impulsive' short planning horizon and current sleep debt make socializing feel like a net negative right now. I'll handle the avoidance of friction by sending a quick text later, rather than committing to go out which would drain my focus capacity needed for the exam.

desires: Finish the last 40% of the midterm review before midnight so I don't have to face a bad grade tomorrow.
Avoid having to explain to Mina and Jae why I'm ghosting their plans again tonight.
Get through this hour without my brain completely locking up from the sleep debt.
